name: Orange Pineapple Praline Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 can 20 oz well drained canned crushed pineapple-reserve
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease and flour two 9-inch round cake pans. Set aside.
2. Prepare the yellow cake mix according to package directions using the 2 eggs and substituting orange juice for water listed in the directions. Stir in grated orange peel into the cake batter; divide one-fourth of the batter among each pan, spreading half of the pineapple mixture on top. Pour remaining batter over the pineapple and place pans on the center rack in the oven. Bake for 30-35 minutes or until a gentle press with a fingertip leaves no indentation.
3. Prepare the praline topping: In a small bowl, toss coconut, brown sugar, and nuts with melted butter. Remove the cake layers from the oven. Spoon the praline mixture onto one layer; return to the oven for 10 minutes longer. Let the second layer cool slightly on the rack before transferring it completely to cool.
4. After cooling, place a piece of foil about 12 inches square on the wire rack. Carefully transfer the cooled cake layers onto the foil. Put heavy cream in a small bowl.
5. Beat cream on high until stiff peaks form when beaters are lifted. Spread half the whipped cream over the plain layer. Add the praline-topped layer, then frost the sides with remaining cream. Pipe rosettes and place cherries in the center of each rosette. Refrigerate until ready to serve.
Serves: 10-12
Source: McCalls Cooking School
